item.title: Version 1.0
item.description:
• Initial release of WireTunnels
  • Manage WireGuard tunnels from the menu bar and dashboard
  • Privileged helper for secure tunnel management
  • Import, create, and edit tunnel configurations
  • Auto-connect on network changes

item.title: Version 1.0.1
item.description: • Fixed privileged helper detection: the app could report the helper as "not installed" or stuck requiring approval even when it was actually enabled, because status was read from a long-lived cached handle instead of a fresh system query

item.title: Version 1.0.2
item.description:
• Shared tunnels: administrators can create tunnels visible to every user on the Mac, control who's allowed to connect or disconnect, and see who's currently connected
  • On-Demand rules can now connect and disconnect on different Wi-Fi networks independently, instead of a single action per tunnel
  • Pick Wi-Fi networks for On-Demand rules from a list of known networks, or type one manually
  • Shared tunnel changes now ask for your admin password once per session instead of every time
  • Added a "Hide Dock Icon" option in Settings for menu-bar-only use
  • Fixed On-Demand rules for specific Wi-Fi networks not triggering (this needed Location permission to read the current network name)

item.title: Version 1.0.3
item.description:
• Reliable reconnect after sleep — tunnels now automatically restart after your Mac wakes, instead of the dashboard silently showing "Connected" while traffic has actually stopped
  • Per-tunnel kill switch — block all other network traffic if a tunnel drops unexpectedly, until it reconnects or you turn it off; works for personal tunnels and for shared tunnels (administrator-controlled)
  • QR code import/export — show a tunnel as a QR code to pair a phone with the official WireGuard app in seconds, or import a tunnel by dropping, opening, or pasting a QR code image
  • Live throughput sparkline + connection test — each tunnel row now shows a live throughput sparkline, and the tunnel detail view has an on-demand "Test Connection" button that checks latency to your VPN server

Notes

  • The kill switch enforces its rules via the system packet filter (PF) through the existing privileged helper — no new permissions beyond what's already required
  • The connection test only pings the tunnel's own configured VPN server; it makes no calls to any third-party service, consistent with WireTunnels' no-telemetry design
  • Existing installs will prompt to update the privileged helper on first launch after upgrading (required for the kill switch's new helper capability)
